artPrompt: An ornate hero shot of a battered spinning index-card file standing on a casserole-laden folding table, its fanned blank cards rimmed in soft gold, bound with rubber bands and pierced by a worn pencil. Dramatic afternoon light haloes the humble object as if it were a sacred relic, a few cards drifting loose against a blurred backdrop of picket fences and leafy green. Centered and reverent, composed tall for a 4:3 landscape frame, rendered as a vibrant gouache storybook illustration — saturated matte color, simplified flat-ish shapes, soft painterly edges, decorative.
name: Petunia's Rolodex
text: {1}, {T}: Look at the top card of your library. If it's an Organizer, Volunteer, or Petitioner card, you may reveal it and put it into your hand. Otherwise, create a 1/1 green and white Voter creature token and leave the card there. (Everyone in town owes Petunia a favor. This is the index.)
flavorText: Index cards, rubber bands, and the home number of every councilmember who ever voted wrong. Lethal in the right hands.
